title: Sales History and Returns
overall_goal: To provide instructions on how to navigate the Sales History section, locate specific transactions, modify payment methods, and process returns in Linko-POS Desktop.
rag_description: This documentation section provides a comprehensive guide on managing sales records within the Linko-POS Desktop 'History' module. It covers the process of accessing the Sales sub-menu, utilizing filters such as synchronization status and date ranges, and using search functions by receipt number or customer name. It details how to view transaction specifics (items, warehouse, totals) and perform critical actions: changing payment types (cash, terminal, or debt), re-printing receipts, and executing the return process. The return workflow explained includes selecting specific items from a sale, validating available quantities, choosing refund methods, and understanding how the system automatically updates inventory levels and cash register accounting upon completion.

content.description: From the left-hand menu, navigate to the 'History' section and select 'Sales'. The window that opens displays the list of all receipts. At the top, you will find filters for 'Synchronized / Not Synchronized', the main receipt list in the center, and a date range selector in the top-right corner.

By default, receipts are displayed based on a specific time period. Click the date in the top right to select the required range (e.g., the specific month or day the receipt was issued). If the customer has provided a receipt number or name, you can quickly locate the order using the 'Search' field at the top.

Click on a specific receipt row to open the 'Order No...' details window. Here you can view the customer name, creation date, warehouse, total amount, and the price/quantity for each item. Available actions include: 'Change payment' (to switch between cash, terminal, or debt without changing items/totals), 'Return' (to initiate a item return), and 'Issue Receipt' / 'Print' to resend the receipt to the printer.

Click the 'Return' button to open the 'Return Order No...' window. Items from the original order are shown on the right with an 'Available: X' count. Click items on the right to move them to the return cart on the left. Adjust quantities using +/- ensuring they do not exceed the available amount. Click the bottom 'Return' button, select the refund method (Cash, Terminal, or Deduct from Customer Debt), and click 'Give money'. The system will automatically return items to stock and record the expense in the cash register.

If you cannot find a specific receipt, first verify the date range settings. Ensure the selected period in History corresponds exactly to the day the receipt was issued. For example, if the receipt was issued last month, you must select last month's date range rather than the current month.

title: Return History
overall_goal: Manage and review the history of returned goods and print return receipts in Linko-POS Desktop.
rag_description: This documentation guide describes how to use the Return History feature in the Linko-POS Desktop software. It covers navigating to the 'Returns' section within the 'History' menu, where users can view a comprehensive list of return transactions including receipt numbers, client info, and total amounts. The guide explains how to view detailed itemized data for each return, reprint return receipts for customers, and utilize powerful filtering tools like the date range selector and search bar (capable of searching by customer name, phone, or receipt ID) to manage and audit returned inventory efficiently.

content.description: Open the main menu on the left side of the screen and select the 'Returns' item under the 'History' section. This displays a list of all return receipts for the selected period. The table contains information such as the Receipt Number (return ID), Client (whom the goods were received from), Amount (returned sum), and Date and Time of the operation.

content.step: 2. Viewing Return Details and Printing Receipts
content.description: Click on any row in the table to open the full details of that specific return, showing the product list, quantities, prices, and the total amount. To generate a physical copy of the return receipt, click the 'Print receipt' button located at the bottom of the details window.

To filter results by time, click the date filter in the top right of the screen, select the desired start and end dates in the calendar window, and click 'Apply' to refresh the list. You can also use the 'Search' field at the top to quickly locate a specific return by entering the customer's name, phone number, or the receipt number.

title: Returns to Suppliers History
overall_goal: To view, filter, and review the history of product returns made to suppliers and print corresponding return documents.
rag_description: This section describes the 'Returns to Suppliers' history feature in the Linko-POS Desktop application. It provides a step-by-step guide for users to access return logs, filter transactions by synchronization status and date ranges, search for specific receipts, and view itemized details of each return transaction. Key functionalities covered include navigating the 'History' menu, interpreting the list of returned items (receipt number, supplier, amount, timestamp), and printing return receipts for physical documentation. This module is essential for tracking supply chain reversals and reconciling accounts with vendors.

content.step: Step 1. Accessing the 'History → Return to Suppliers' section
content.description: From the menu on the left side, select the 'History' section and then choose 'Return to Suppliers'.

content.step: Step 2. Viewing the list of returns
content.description: A window will display all records of returned goods. The table columns include: Receipt Number (the return ID), Supplier (the recipient of the goods), Amount (the total value), and Date and Time (the timestamp of the return). You can quickly find a specific receipt using the 'Synchronized / Not Synchronized' tabs or the 'Search' field. Additionally, you can filter the records by clicking the date range in the top right and selecting a specific period from the calendar.

content.step: Step 3. Viewing return details and printing
content.description: Clicking on a specific receipt in the table will expand detailed information below the row, such as the product name, quantity, unit price, and total sum. You can also print the return receipt by clicking the 'Print receipt' button located at the bottom of the screen.

title: Supply History
overall_goal: To view and analyze the records of incoming goods (supplies) and adjustments made during inventory revisions.
rag_description: This documentation section explains how to manage and review the Supply History within the Linko-POS Desktop system. It covers navigating to the History module, understanding the data table structure, and utilizing filters like date ranges, synchronization status, and search tools. Key features include distinguishing between regular supplier deliveries (Oprichodovanie) and stock adjustments (Inventory), as well as viewing granular details for each transaction such as cost price, selling price, profit margins, and specific item quantities. This section serves as a central hub for auditing warehouse movements and verifying supplier settlements.

content.description: Navigate to the 'History' section using the left-hand sidebar menu.

content.step: Step 2: Table Structure and Navigation Filters
content.description: The table includes columns for Receipt Number, Supplier, Warehouse, Date and Time, and Total Amount. Control elements at the top allow you to filter by synchronization status (online server sync), a search field for receipt numbers or supplier names, and a date range filter on the right to select specific periods.

If a specific name (e.g., Nestle, Lipton) appears in the Supplier column, it indicates a standard stock-in (Oprichodovanie) from a real supplier. If 'Inventory' (Инвентаризация) is listed, the entry was automatically generated during a stock audit to adjust inventory levels (e.g., correcting a 0 balance to 10 units).

Clicking any row in the table expands a detailed block below. This view displays receipt details, warehouse info, total supply cost, selling price, margin percentage, and expected profit. A secondary table lists every item in the shipment, showing its name, quantity, purchase/selling prices, and total sum.

content.step: Step 5: Working with Dates and Search Tools
content.description: Use the date range selector to narrow down supplies to specific months or weeks. The Search field provides quick access via receipt numbers or text fragments. Use the 'Synchronized / Not Synchronized' tabs to distinguish between supplies already uploaded to the server and those stored locally offline.

The History -> Supplies section allows for centralized control of all supplier entries, inventory revision results, and profit margin analysis. It is the primary source of information for analyzing warehouse movements, identifying errors, and checking financial settlements with suppliers.

title: Revision History
overall_goal: To view and analyze the history of inventory revisions (audits) performed in the warehouse, including stock differences and who conducted them.
rag_description: This guide explains how to access and analyze the Revision History within the Linko-POS Desktop system. Users learn to navigate to the 'History' -> 'Revision' section to see a list of all inventory audits conducted. The section provides details such as receipt numbers, responsible users, warehouse locations, timestamps, and total financial differences (surplus or deficit). It also covers viewing granular details for specific revisions—comparing 'before' and 'after' stock levels for individual items—and using date range filters to locate specific inventory checks within a chosen time period. This feature is essential for warehouse transparency and auditing.

content.step: Step 1. Accessing the Revision History section
content.description: Select "History" → "Revision" from the left menu. A list of all inventory revisions performed across the warehouses will appear here.

The table displays the following columns: Receipt Number (revision ID), User (who performed the revision), Warehouse (the specific location), Date and Time (when it was conducted), and Total Amount (the overall financial difference resulting from the revision). A plus amount means items were added, while a minus amount indicates items were reduced.

Clicking on any row opens the full details of that revision. The top section shows the receipt number, user, warehouse, and total sum. The table below lists each item with: "Before" (stock balance before revision), "After" (stock balance after revision), and "Difference" (the change in quantity and value). This allows you to see exactly which items had discrepancies.

Click on the date range in the top right part of the screen and select the desired period from the calendar. By changing the period, only the revisions made within that time frame will remain in the list, while others will be hidden. This helps in quickly checking inventory history for a specific month or week.

title: Shift History Management
overall_goal: To view, track, search, and print reports of past work shifts within the Linko-POS system.
rag_description: This section guides users through the 'Shift History' module in Linko-POS Desktop. It details how to navigate the shift list, which includes critical financial data such as starting balances, income, expenses, expected vs. actual cash totals, and closing discrepancies. The instructions cover viewing expanded details for specific shifts, using the search functionality to filter records by user or transaction amount, and utilizing the print feature to generate physical or PDF reports. This is essential for financial auditing and monitoring cashier performance over time.

content.step: Step 1. Accessing the Shift History section
content.description: Select the 'History' -> 'Shifts' sections from the left-side menu. This will open a list of all recorded shifts, displaying the shift number, assigned user, creation date, and various financial metrics: 'At the beginning' (starting balance), 'Income', 'Expense', 'Expected' amount, 'Actual' amount, 'Difference' (discrepancy), and the 'Closing date'.

Click once on the row of the shift you wish to examine. A detailed information pane will open below, showing the specific user, the exact opening and closing dates, and a breakdown for each cash register including: 'At the beginning', 'Income', 'Expense', 'Expected', 'Actual', and 'Difference'.

Use the 'Search' bar at the top to find a specific shift by name, user, or amount. To generate a report, click the 'Print' button in the details window to output the shift summary in paper or PDF format.
